senses.definition: Having wasted away or being unusually small or weak because of lack of growth or use.
examples: The disease left him with atrophous muscles in his legs.
Atrophous limbs are a common result of long periods of immobility.

senses.definition: A gradual wasting away or loss of size and strength in a part of the body, or in a skill, system, or feeling because it is not used.
examples: Being in a cast for months can cause muscle atrophy in the leg.
Without practice, his language skills fell into atrophy.

senses.definition: To gradually waste away, or to become weaker and less effective because it is not used or supported.
examples: If you never exercise, your muscles will atrophy.
Their creativity began to atrophy in the strict corporate environment.

senses.definition: Present participle of atrophy: wasting away or becoming weaker and less effective.
examples: The doctor warned that the unused limb was atrophying.
In many towns, local traditions are atrophying as young people move away.

senses.definition: Poisoning or a state caused by too much atropine in the body.
examples: The patient showed classic signs of atropia after the overdose.
Atropia can lead to confusion, dry mouth, and rapid heartbeat.

senses.definition: Plural of atropia: several cases or types of poisoning caused by too much atropine.
examples: The report described multiple atropias linked to improper dosing.
Clinicians discussed the atropias they had managed that year.

senses.definition: Relating to or affected by atropine or a similar substance.
examples: The atropic effects included dry skin and dilated pupils.
Researchers studied the atropic reactions in laboratory animals.

senses.definition: A scientific family name used in zoology or taxonomy, typically referring to a specific group of organisms.
examples: The species was once classified under Atropidae.
Atropidae is mentioned in older taxonomic literature.

senses.definition: Another form of the word atropine, a drug used to treat certain poisonings and slow heart rate problems.
examples: Atropin is administered in emergencies to counter nerve agent exposure.
The nurse prepared a dose of atropin for the patient with severe bradycardia.

senses.definition: A strong medicine that blocks certain nerve signals and is used to treat some types of poisoning, slow heart rate, and to widen the pupils of the eye.
examples: Paramedics gave atropine to treat the organophosphate poisoning.
Atropine eye drops are used to dilate the pupils during an eye exam.

senses.definition: Plural of atropine: different forms or doses of atropine, or several instances of its use.
examples: The study compared various atropines in treating bradycardia.
Pharmacists checked the storage conditions of all atropines in the emergency cart.

senses.definition: A condition caused by taking too much atropine, with symptoms like dry mouth, fast heartbeat, and confusion.
examples: The patient developed atropinism after receiving repeated high doses.
Typical signs of atropinism include flushed skin and dilated pupils.

senses.definition: The medical process of giving atropine until its full effects appear in the patient.
examples: In nerve agent exposure, rapid atropinization is often necessary.
The team monitored the patient closely during atropinization.

senses.definition: To treat a person with atropine until the typical effects of the drug appear.
examples: Doctors had to atropinize the victim to counteract the nerve gas.
They carefully atropinized the patient while watching for side effects.

senses.definition: Plural form of atropin, referring to different preparations or doses of atropine.
examples: The emergency kit contained several atropins for immediate use.
Nurses must check expiry dates on all stored atropins.

senses.definition: A tendency of certain living tissues to react in a special way to the drug atropine.
examples: Researchers examined atropism in different muscle tissues.
Atropism can vary widely between species.

senses.definition: Plural of atropism: several types or examples of special reactions to atropine.
examples: The paper described different atropisms seen in cardiac tissues.
Various atropisms were observed across the test animals.

senses.definition: In Greek mythology, the Fate who cuts the thread of life, deciding when a person dies.
examples: In the myth, Atropos cuts the thread that ends each life.
Artists often show Atropos holding a pair of shears.
collocations: Atropos and the thread of life

senses.definition: In botany, describing a type of ovule that is straight, with the micropyle at the end opposite the stalk.
examples: The plant produces atropous ovules with a simple structure.
Atropous ovules differ in shape from curved types.

senses.definition: Dark red or blackish-red in color.
examples: The atrorubent clouds glowed above the setting sun.
He chose an atrorubent fabric for the dramatic costume.

senses.definition: Dark blood-red in color.
examples: An atrosanguineous streak marked the edge of the horizon.
The painter mixed pigments to create an atrosanguineous shade.

senses.definition: A poisonous substance similar to atropine, found in certain plants.
examples: Atroscine, like atropine, can affect the nervous system.
The researchers isolated atroscine from the plant extract.

senses.definition: Very black or dark in color.
examples: Her atrous hair fell over her shoulders.
The night was atrous, without a single visible star.

senses.definition: Without holes or perforations.
examples: The shell is described as atry in early zoological texts.
An atry plate was used to prevent any leakage.

senses.definition: The name of a genus of extinct marine animals (brachiopods) known from fossils.
examples: Atrypa is a common fossil in some Paleozoic rocks.
Students examined an Atrypa specimen in the geology lab.

senses.definition: Having a form similar to that of the fossil genus Atrypa.
examples: The fossil showed an atrypoid outline.
Researchers described the shell as atrypoid in shape.

senses.definition: An abbreviation that can stand for different technical or organizational names, depending on context.
examples: The company upgraded its ATS to handle more job applications.
Please check with ATS for the latest schedule.

senses.definition: A Filipino pickled green papaya side dish, often served with grilled or fried foods.
examples: They served grilled fish with atsara on the side.
Her homemade atsara was sweet, sour, and crunchy.

senses.definition: A short form used in various technical and business contexts, whose full meaning depends on the field.
examples: The att column in the report shows attendance.
Please send the documents to the address att the finance department.

senses.definition: Wholemeal wheat flour used especially in South Asian cooking.
examples: She bought a bag of atta to make fresh chapatis.
Atta is commonly used for flatbreads in Indian households.

senses.definition: An enthusiastic phrase used to praise someone, especially a child, for doing something well.
examples: “Attababy!” the coach shouted when the child scored.
Her father smiled and said, “Attababy, you did it.”

senses.definition: A large drum, especially one used in military or ceremonial music.
examples: The deep sound of the attabal echoed across the square.
Soldiers marched to the beat of the attabal.

senses.definition: An informal phrase used to show strong approval, especially to a boy or man, for something he has done.
examples: “Attaboy!” his friends cheered when he finished the race.
Dad patted him on the back and said, “Attaboy, I’m proud of you.”

senses.definition: Informal expressions of praise, especially directed at boys or men.
examples: He was tired of empty attaboys and wanted real support.
The coach handed out attaboys after the big win.

senses.definition: A name historically used for an Indigenous people or language of the Gulf Coast region of the United States.
examples: Early explorers wrote about meeting the Attacapan.
Linguists have studied what remains of the Attacapan language.

senses.definition: In music, an instruction to start the next section or movement immediately, without a pause.
examples: The score marked the second movement attacca.
The conductor signaled attacca into the finale.

senses.definition: In music, the entry or beginning of a theme, or the way a passage is attacked or started.
examples: The attacco of the violins must be very precise.
Composers often mark the attacco carefully in the score.

senses.definition: To join or fix one thing to another.
examples: Please attach your photo to the application form.
She attached the cable to the back of the computer.

senses.definition: To connect a feeling, importance, or condition to something.
examples: People often attach great importance to exam results.
He tends to attach too much meaning to small details.

senses.definition: That can be fixed or joined to something else.
examples: The vacuum cleaner comes with several attachable heads.
She bought a lamp with an attachable clamp.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to be attached or joined.
examples: The attachableness of the device makes it very flexible.
Engineers tested the attachableness of each module.

senses.definition: A person who works at an embassy, usually with a special area of responsibility.
examples: She worked as a cultural attache at the embassy.
The military attache attended the official ceremony.

senses.definition: Joined or fixed to something.
examples: Please see the attached document for more details.
The garage is attached to the side of the house.

senses.definition: Feeling strong affection or connection to someone or something.
examples: She is very attached to her grandparents.
He became attached to the city during his stay there.

senses.definition: In a way that shows strong emotional attachment.
examples: He spoke very attachedly of his childhood home.
She looked attachedly at the old family photographs.

senses.definition: A person or device that attaches or fixes something to something else.
examples: The company hired an attacher to fasten labels on the products.
Each stapler acts as a simple paper attacher.

senses.definition: People or devices that attach or fix things to other things.
examples: Automatic attachers speed up the packaging process.
In the factory, several attachers work along the assembly line.

senses.definition: Plural of attache: people working at an embassy with special duties.
examples: Several military attaches attended the conference.
The cultural attaches organized an art exhibition.

senses.definition: Third person singular of attach: joins or fixes one thing to another.
examples: She attaches the files to the email before sending.
The device automatically attaches the tag to each bag.

senses.definition: The position or period of service of an attache.
examples: Her attacheship in Paris lasted three years.
He wrote a book about his wartime attacheship.

senses.definition: Present participle of attach: joining or fixing one thing to another.
examples: She is attaching the documents to the email now.
Workers were busy attaching new signs to the building.

senses.definition: A thing that is joined to something else, especially a piece of equipment added to a machine.
examples: This blender comes with several attachments.
He used a special attachment on his drill to cut the circle.

senses.definition: A file that is sent with an email.
examples: Please check the attachment for the full report.
I forgot to add the attachment before sending the email.

senses.definition: A strong feeling of love or connection to a person, place, or thing.
examples: She has a deep attachment to her hometown.
His attachment to his pet was obvious to everyone.

senses.definition: Plural of attachment: extra parts for machines or devices.
examples: The kitchen machine has many attachments for different tasks.
He bought several attachments for his camera.

senses.definition: Plural of attachment: files sent with emails.
examples: Please see the attachments for more information.
She forgot to include the attachments in her message.

senses.definition: Plural of attachment: strong feelings of love or connection to people or things.
examples: Children often form strong attachments to their caregivers.
Over the years, he developed deep attachments to his students.

senses.definition: To use force to hurt a person, animal, or place, often with weapons.
examples: The army planned to attack at dawn.
The dog suddenly attacked the man.

senses.definition: To strongly criticize someone or something in speech or writing.
examples: The politician attacked the new law in his speech.
The article attacks the company for its poor safety record.

senses.definition: An act of using force to hurt someone or damage something.
examples: The bombing was the worst attack in years.
He reported the attack to the police.

senses.definition: A sudden medical problem, such as a heart attack or asthma attack.
examples: He was taken to hospital after a heart attack.
She had an asthma attack during the night.
